nodes.label: P4.1 — ambient-dim tolerance (A2)
nodes.rationale: OURS's first clear WIN: sweeping ambient nuisance dimension [8..500] at fixed difficulty, OURS holds ~0.79-0.85 while tuned BP declines and Mono-Forward collapses toward chance; gap-to-BP crosses zero by dim~350-400.

nodes.label: A2 — nuisance-dimension robustness (OURS's first clear win)
nodes.rationale: The contrastive objective down-weights non-discriminative ambient dimensions for free (a nuisance dim is identical across a sample's positive/negative contrast views, contributing ~nothing to InfoNCE); OURS crosses above tuned BP by dim 500 and is also cheaper — the substrate-relevant win since analog crossbars are wide/high-fan-in.

nodes.label: Mono-Forward flagged high-D-unsafe
nodes.rationale: Mono-Forward has no nuisance filter (every input dim feeds the per-layer supervised CE), so nuisance dims become spurious memorized features -> catastrophic overfitting (gen-gap 0.59) -> collapse toward chance at high dim; keep it as the cautionary racer, not a safe high-D reference.

nodes.label: Crossover dim is difficulty-dependent (map coordinate for P5)
nodes.rationale: The dim at which OURS overtakes tuned BP shifts with task difficulty; flagged as a key map coordinate needing a difficulty x dim heatmap in P4.2/synthesis — wide analog inputs are OURS's friend, not its foe.
